title: Three VLCCs loading Russian Urals crude at Novorossiysk for India delivery above G7 price cap
summary: Oil shipping analyst OilCfd tracked three VLCCs loading Russian Urals crude at Novorossiysk, with charter rates and cargo pricing indicating transactions above the G7 $60/barrel price cap, destined for Indian refineries in Gujarat.
contents:
# Three VLCCs Loading Russian Crude Above G7 Price Cap

## Tracking Report
Oil shipping analyst OilCfd identified three Very Large Crude Carriers (VLCCs) loading Russian Urals crude at Novorossiysk Black Sea port.

## Vessel Details
- **OCEAN NAVIGATOR** (IMO 9345678) - 300k DWT, Liberian flag, recently renamed
- **STAR FORTUNE** (IMO 9456789) - 310k DWT, Cameroon flag, age 18 years
- **PACIFIC GLORY** (IMO 9567890) - 295k DWT, Gabon flag, age 20 years

## Cargo and Pricing
- Combined cargo: approximately 6 million barrels Urals crude
- Estimated loading price: $68-72/barrel (above G7 $60 cap)
- Charter rates: premium of $8-10/barrel above market (sanctions risk premium)
- Destination: Jamnagar and Vadinar refineries, Gujarat, India
- All three vessels lack Western P&I club insurance

## Sanctions Circumvention Indicators
- All three vessels renamed within past 12 months
- Flags of convenience from non-G7 aligned nations
- Insurance provided by Russian or unknown providers
- Pricing above G7 price cap violates sanctions framework

## Market Context
- Russian crude exports to India increased 15% year-over-year
- India now processes approximately 2.1 million bpd of Russian crude
- Shadow fleet capacity expanding to accommodate growing trade volumes
- G7 price cap enforcement remains limited due to lack of maritime jurisdiction
